text: In software development, Git branching is essential for team collaboration. A version control workflow typically begins with a main branch, where stable code lives. When working on a new feature, developers create a separate feature branch from the main branch. This allows them to experiment and make changes without affecting the stable codebase. After completing the feature, they create a pull request to propose their changes. Other team members review the code and discuss improvements. Once approved, the feature branch is merged back into the main branch through a merging process. This workflow keeps the project organized, prevents conflicts, and ensures that only tested code is added to the final product.
